executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with over 100 Canadian organizations publicly victimized in a single 48-hour period. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (with confirmed database leak), and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 18 victims, followed by Clop with 13 and Play with 9. Critical infrastructure sectors including healthcare, education, public sector entities, and financial services are heavily impacted, suggesting a coordinated or opportunistic mass exploitation event targeting Canadian organizations.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 18 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Waterloo Information Systems, BioPharma Services, Workers Health & Safety Centre, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), and multiple manufacturing, logistics, and professional services firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology companies (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions), manufacturing (Ideal Welders), hospitality (Whiskijack Resorts), and business services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - known for mass exploitation campaigns

items.name: Play
items.targets: 9 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, and multiple manufacturing and financial services firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information)

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Teccart College (education), construction firms, manufacturing, and transportation/logistics companies
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, Esposito Bros Construction, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, and Emond Publishing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: The Gentlemen
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac, ControlGMC (manufacturing), and Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, and KTL Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing)

items.name: Incransom
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including healthcare (Bison Family Medical), manufacturing (Steelworks Inc, Omega Tool Corp), and construction sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery)

items.name: Sinobi

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: AiLock

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a national-scale ransomware emergency with 100+ organizations victimized across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical sectors hit include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, multiple medical facilities), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collège Supérieur de Montréal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth), and public health (Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre). No CCCS advisories identified in this dataset.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose attacked by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and Shinyhunters). Active Green + Ross and Kids & Company (consumer services) also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with confirmed database leak. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting and financial services firms affected including Pewarchuk CPA and MG Chartered Professional Accountant.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation in the wild, demanding immediate patching. A critical Cisco ISE vulnerability (CVE-2026-20147) allows authenticated remote code execution with a severity of 9.9. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued an advisory for Fortinet FortiClientEMS CVE-2026-35616. Akira ransomware has breached Nobu Restaurant Group, leading to a data breach reported to Texas authorities. A new AI-powered vishing platform called ATHR is enabling automated voice-based phishing attacks at scale.

items.description: Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability under active exploitation
Cisco ISE and Cisco ISE-PIC authenticated remote code execution vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: FastGPT AI Agent platform password-based login endpoint vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: FastGPT AI Agent platform password change endpoint vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Nordic Semiconductor IronSide SE for nRF54H20 algorithmic complexity issue
items.software_affected: Nordic Semiconductor IronSide SE for nRF54H20 before 23.0.2+17
items.cia_impact: Availability: High, Confidentiality: Low, Integrity: Low

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Nobu Restaurant Group
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Agent Tesla
items.targets: General targeting - 16 new samples detected
items.ttps: T1056 (Input Capture), T1113 (Screen Capture), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol)

items.name: ATHR Vishing Platform
items.targets: General population - automated AI voice phishing attacks
items.ttps: T1566.004 (Phishing: Spear Phishing Voice), T1583 (Acquire Infrastructure), AI-driven social engineering

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued Alert AL26-007 regarding Fortinet FortiClientEMS vulnerability CVE-2026-35616, indicating heightened concern for Canadian organizations using this product. No specific incidents targeting Canadian retail or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period. RCMP issued a statement of regret for Indigenous surveillance programs, reflecting ongoing trust concerns in Canadian law enforcement cybersecurity practices.
